messages.content: Mission target: <TARGET_IP>. Begin the engagement now. Spawn the appropriate first agent.

{"thought": "SITUATION: Target <TARGET_IP> identified. Mission objective: find flag.txt. Current phase: OSINT. Target is an internal IP (192.168.x.x), limiting traditional OSINT value. The most effective first step is active reconnaissance via port scanning to identify running services, which will inform exploitation strategy. Given my playbook shows this IP has been successfully exploited before with vsftpd, samba, distcc, and unrealircd backdoors, I need to confirm what's currently exposed. HYPOTHESIS: This appears to be a vulnerable Linux target (legacy-vulnerable-vm style). Port scan will reveal exploitable services. DECISION: Spawn a scanner agent to perform comprehensive port enumeration (TCP + UDP) and service version detection.", "action": "spawn_agent", "parameters": {"agent_type": "scanner", "target": "<TARGET_IP>", "task_type": "scan_tcp_udp_full", "options": {"ports": "1-10000", "detect_versions": true, "os_detect": true}}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: Scanner agent scanner-08efa364 has been spawned targeting <TARGET_IP>. Task is TCP/UDP full port scan with version detection. The scan will take some time to complete (ports 1-10000). HYPOTHESIS: Scanner will reveal open ports and service versions. Based on my playbook for this target IP, previous successful exploits include vsftpd, samba, distcc, and unrealircd — all of which suggest this is a legacy vulnerable Linux target. DECISION: Wait for scan completion to get exact service versions before attempting exploits. Per my methodology: 'know everything before you exploit anything.'", "action": "wait_for_agents", "parameters": {"agent_ids": ["scanner-08efa364"], "timeout": 1200}}
